source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Senior Full Stack Engineer, you will be a hands-on contributor to the frontend and backend apps powering our platform. You will learn Elixir on the job from your colleagues and technical leaders. You will collaborate with technical leaders and the founding team to deliver new features to our application. You will work closely with your colleagues to refactor and improve the codebase; create a beautiful, functional, and delightful user experience; and build an excellent and supportive engineering culture.



Please note: Hiive does not use Ruby or Ruby on Rails. We do not have Ruby code in production now nor plan to do so in the future. This job is open to Ruby developers who have a desire to learn and work in Elixir and Phoenix. We will teach you both on the job and expect you to invest in self-learning to grow your skills.



In this role, your responsibilities will include:

 - Consider themselves highly capable at hands-on development of features in both the frontend and backend and be able to ship React/Typescript code on day one.

 - Possess the know how to build customer-facing products across the entire stack, from the data model to the UI.

 - Have a strong desire to learn Elixir and experience picking up new programming languages.

 - Be proactive and self-motivated and comfortable learning on the job in a fast-paced environment.

 - Be comfortable working with a small team including directly with the founders on building a product and a company.

 - Adapt well to a quickly moving environment.



Required Experience:

 - Substantial expertise building interfaces with React and working in Typescript.

 - Strong experience building backend API services using Ruby and Ruby on Rails.

 - Have a philosophy in writing automated backend tests.

 - Translating design mockups (e.g. Figma) into frontend implementation.

 - Working closely with product designers on feedback for UI/UX changes.



Preferred Experience:

 - Understanding of the ins and outs of data modeling and software architecture for developing performant and maintainable code.

 - Experience building or working with GraphQL APIs.

 - Working closely with product managers on understanding use cases, prioritizing features, and scoping work.

 - Exposure to Elixir or other functional languages.



Compensation, Benefits & Perks: 

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing early-stage startup through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health and dental premiums, a health and personal spending account.

 - A dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity. Open to Canada and US-based remote candidates.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

 - An engaging social calendar, including bi-weekly catered lunches, bi-weekly “Friday bar,” team workouts, annual summer party, and holiday party, two “onsite” all-team retreats each year, semi-annual team-building events, and Hiive Womens’ Network events.

 - Significant opportunities for growth into team leadership and m

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Elixir
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Senior Engineer at Hiive, you will be a hands-on contributor to the backed system powering our platform, while building a user friendly interface. You will collaborate with technical leadership to deliver new core features to our application. You will work closely with your colleagues to refactor and improve the codebase; create a robust, scalable and secure infrastructure; and build an excellent and supportive engineering culture.



In this role, your responsibilities will include:

 - Consider themselves highly capable at hands-on development of Elixir, Phoenix, and Ecto from day one.

 - Strong experience working on frontend user interfaces

 - Know the ins and outs of data modeling and software architecture for developing performant and maintainable code.

 - Have experience with integrating with third party services through APIs and webhooks alongside local data processing.

 - Be well versed in all aspects of secure API development.

 - Be comfortable working with a small team including directly with the founders on building a product and a company.

 - Adapt well to a quickly moving environment.



Required Experience:

 - Substantial expertise building backend systems with Elixir and Phoenix.

 - Strong experience utilizing Typescript and React.

 - Able to work in a team environment with a CI/CD driven workflow.

 - Have a philosophy in writing automated backend tests.

 - Strong English language skills.



Preferable Experience:

 - Experience building GraphQL APIs using Absinthe.

 - Understanding of Postgres databases design and optimization.

 - Previous experience working in a fintech startup
   
   

Compensation, Benefits & Perks: 

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing early-stage startup through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health and dental premiums, a health and personal spending account.

 - A dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

 - An engaging social calendar, including bi-weekly catered lunches, bi-weekly “Friday bar,” team workouts, annual summer party, and holiday party, two “onsite” all-team retreats each year, semi-annual team-building events, and Hiive Womens’ Network events.

 - Significant opportunities for growth into team leadership and management roles.

 - Entrepreneurial culture and a small and dynamic team.

 - Sponsorship, immigration, and relocation for exceptional candidates.

Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge sto

source_jobs.title: Site Reliability Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Site Reliability Engineer at Hiive, you will be responsible for ensuring the reliability, availability, and performance of our platform. You’ll join our small but growing infrastructure team, working closely with the DevOps team and engineering leadership. As a hands-on contributor, you will build scalable and resilient infrastructure, automate processes, and respond to incidents efficiently and effectively.



You will help implement security and compliance measures, act as a trusted resource to your colleagues, and collaborate across teams to continuously improve our platform’s performance and reliability. You’ll also contribute to fostering an excellent, supportive engineering culture.



As Hiive continues to expand its use of AI across the platform, you will play a key role in building and operating the infrastructure that powers these systems. This includes supporting AI/ML workloads, improving observability into model performance and system behavior, and ensuring these services are reliable, scalable, and cost-efficient in production.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Maintain and improve our platform's uptime and availability

 - Optimize and maintain our infrastructure to improve reliability, performance, and security

 - Proactively identify and resolve scaling and reliability issues before they impact users or business metrics

 - Partner with product engineers to troubleshoot performance issues and implement effective solutions

 - Configure and maintain monitoring, alerting, and observability systems across our stack

 - Assist with incident response, including investigation, mitigation, and postmortems; develop and maintain incident runbooks

 - Participate in an on-call rotation shared across the engineering organization

 - Support and scale infrastructure for AI/ML systems, including model-serving workloads, data pipelines, and batch/async processing

 - Improve observability for AI systems (latency, cost, drift, failures) and help define reliability standards for these workloads

 - 

Required Skills:

 - Experience in a Site Reliability Engineering or similar role

 - Experience working with (writing or deploying) Elixir, or a strong desire to learn

 - Experience operating production Kubernetes clusters

 - Proficiency building infrastructure with Terraform

 - Strong experience with AWS (especially EKS, RDS, and VPC) and Vercel

 - Experience working with and optimizing PostgreSQL

 - Experience with Datadog or similar observability tools



Preferred Skills:

 - Experience working in regulated or high-compliance environments

 - Experience with CI/CD systems such as GitHub Actions

 - Experience supporting SOC 2 or similar certifications

 - Experience working with Cloudflare

 - Hands-on development experience in one or more programming languages

 - Experience supporting AI/ML systems in production (e.g., model serving, vector databases, or data pipelines)
   
   

Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal

source_jobs.title: Director of Product Design
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



We are seeking a Director of Product Design to own the user experience and interface design of the platform by recruiting and leading a team of in-house product designers. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in shaping the user experience and driving innovation. This is a unique opportunity to create design excellence at a high-growth startup and deliver experiences that make a meaningful impact on our customers and business.



In this role, your responsibilities will include:

 - Defining and championing the product design vision, ensuring alignment with business goals and user needs.

 - Managing the use and enhancement of a cohesive, scalable design system process, ensuring consistency across products and platforms, maximizing re-use and development velocity.

 - Mentoring, guiding, and inspiring a team of product designers, fostering a culture of creativity, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

 - Working with other members of product leadership to design processes that integrate product management, design, and engineering as part of the SDLC.

 - Facilitating workshops and design reviews with stakeholders, driving alignment across teams and departments.

 - Leading the creation of intuitive, visually compelling, and functional designs for web and mobile platforms.

 - Ensuring all designs meet accessibility, responsiveness, and scalability standards.

 - Staying up-to-date on industry trends, tools, and best practices, incorporating new insights into the design process.

 - Advocating for user-centered design and the value of design as a key driver of business success.



Required Qualifications

 - 10+ years of experience in product design, with a strong portfolio demonstrating innovative and impactful design solutions.

 - Proven leadership experience managing and mentoring design teams.

 - Expert level in working with an in-house created design system that was executed consistently across design files and code bases.

 - History of designing scalable user research and usability processes.

 - Considerable experience working with Figma.

 - Strong communication skills with the ability to articulate design decisions to both technical and non-technical audiences.



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience in SaaS, B2B, or marketplace product environments.

 - Proficiency in working with data and product analytics tools to deliver design insights.

 - Proficiency in UX writing and content strategy.



Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

 - An engaging social calendar, including bi-weekly catered lunches, bi-weekly “Friday

source_jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Sales & Trading
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Senior Analyst in Sales & Trading, you will be a key member of Hiive’s front-line team, working directly with individuals who are looking to buy or sell shares on our platform. In this role, you’ll be a blend of client-facing and operational. You will guide clients through the entire transaction process while ensuring a seamless, compliant, and efficient experience from start to finish.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Serve as the first point of contact for clients, managing their experience and guiding them through the Hiive platform and secondary market.

 - Build trust and rapport with clients by providing clear, actionable market insights.

 - Support the team and customer through the transaction lifecycle, through various channels such as email, phone, or live chat, from initial inquiry and onboarding to execution and closing, ensuring all legal, compliance, and operational requirements are met.

 - Conduct detailed reviews of transaction documentation and platform data to maintain accuracy and minimize errors.

 - Partner with operations, legal, and internal stakeholders to troubleshoot issues and deliver efficient, timely closings.

 - Source and qualify new clients (both buyers and sellers) through outreach, referrals, and platform activity, driving engagement and platform liquidity.

 - Track pipeline activity and transaction metrics with precision, ensuring the desk maintains high standards for speed, compliance, and client satisfaction.

 - Become a Hiive platform expert, seamlessly guiding customers through their journey and providing customer support at any stage.
   
   

Required Experience:

 - A client-focused professional with 3+ years of relevant experience, such as institutional sales/trading, investment banking, private equity, venture capital, investor relations, business development or investment sales.

 - Have experience prospecting and generating leads in a high-volume outreach environment.

 - Demonstrate skills in balancing relationship management with rigorous operational detail, ensuring every transaction is executed flawlessly.

 - Bring a high degree of organization and ruthless prioritization, with the ability to manage multiple client relationships and transaction workflows in a fast-paced environment.

 - Possess strong financial acumen or a demonstrated ability to quickly master complex market dynamics.

 - Have FINRA Series 7 and 63 licenses, or able to obtain shortly after joining.
   

This is an ideal role for someone who thrives on direct client interaction and wants to develop deep market and product expertise in a high-paced transactional environment, while also taking ownership of the operational processes that make every transaction successful.



Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver,

source_jobs.title: Director, Institutional Sales
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As Director, Institutional Sales, you will be a key driver of Hiive’s growth by cultivating and expanding deep relationships with premier global venture capital investors, institutions, and ultra-high-net-worth individuals. You will directly generate significant liquidity and trading volume on our platform by combining strategic relationship management with a relentless sales focus. This is a high-impact, client-facing role where success is measured by revenue, deal flow, and the trust you build in the market.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Becoming an expert on the secondary market for pre-IPO securities, understanding market dynamics, key participants, and the role of Hiive.

 - Developing, owning, and growing a portfolio of institutional and ultra-high-net-worth investor relationships, bringing all of Hiive’s investment capabilities to the market.

 - Driving revenue growth by prospecting new clients, re-engaging dormant accounts, and executing targeted sales campaigns to deliver trading volume and platform adoption.

 - Acting as a trusted relationship manager, delivering consistent, high-value communication and positioning Hiive as the preferred venue for secondary transactions.

 - Expanding Hiive’s market share by opening new channels, identifying fresh pools of investors, and unlocking additional sources of capital.

 - Meeting and exceeding sales performance metrics while cultivating repeat business and long-term client partnerships.
   

The ideal candidate will:

 - Be a highly driven, influential professional with exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, a strong sense of urgency, a superior work ethic, intellectual curiosity, and outstanding organizational abilities.

 - Have a proven track record of success in meeting and exceeding sales targets while building deep, trusted relationships with institutional or ultra-high-net-worth investors.

 - Bring 6+ years of experience in investment sales, mutual fund sales, investment banking, venture capital, private equity, corporate development, or institutional sales and trading.

 - Have a relevant educational background, such as a commerce or economics degree, or exceptional academic performance in a liberal arts or science program.

 - Be FINRA Series 7 and 63 licensed or able to complete the registrations shortly after hiring.



Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

 - An engaging social calendar, including bi-weekly catered lunches, bi-weekly “Friday bar,” team workouts, annual summer party, and holiday party, two “onsite” all-team retreats each

source_jobs.title: Director of Product Management
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



Hiive is looking for a Director of Product Management to lead our product strategy and scale our product organization. Reporting to an industry veteran with 25+ years of experience, you will be a key pillar of our leadership team. You aren’t just a manager; you are a systems thinker who can refine our SDLC, mentor high-performing PMs, and ensure our fast-moving engineering team is shipping the right things to drive exponential business growth.



In this role, your responsibilities will include:

 - Defining and evangelizing the product roadmap, ensuring every feature aligns with Hiive’s long-term business goals and marketplace dynamics.

 - Partnering with the Director of Product Design and Engineering leadership to optimize the SDLC, ensuring a seamless flow from discovery to delivery.

 - Recruiting, mentoring, and scaling a team of Product Managers, fostering a culture of rigorous thinking, data-informed decision-making, and "extreme ownership."

 - Acting as the primary bridge between Product and cross-functional partners in Sales, Marketing, and Operations to ensure successful GTM execution.

 - Implementing and refining mental models for prioritization (e.g., balancing "customer delight" vs. "technical debt" vs. "revenue growth").

 - Establishing a gold standard for how the team uses data to validate hypotheses and measure the ROI of shipped features.



Required Qualifications:

 - 10+ years of Product Management experience, with at least 3+ years in a dedicated leadership or people-management role within a high-growth startup environment.

 - Experience scaling products from early traction to significant market share, specifically within complex ecosystems.

 - Expert-level ability to query data, define KPIs, and use BI tools to drive product strategy (we believe "gut feel" is a starting point, not a destination).

 - The ability to distill complex technical or business trade-offs into clear, actionable narratives for executive stakeholders.

 - Deep understanding of how changes in one part of a system (e.g., a marketplace) ripple through to affect user behavior and business health.



Preferred Qualifications:

 - Deep knowledge of liquidity, clearing, or complex transactional platforms.

 - A background in Software Engineering or a deep technical understanding of modern API-first architectures.

 - Advanced experience with Amplitude, SQL, and Figma to facilitate close collaboration with our Design and Data teams.

 - Experience managing the nuances of high-touch enterprise customers alongside a scalable product vision.
   
   

Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every d

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Product Designer at Hiive, you’ll join a fast-growing, high-impact product team. You’ll work within a modern tech stack alongside strong Design, Product, and Engineering partners who move quickly yet also care deeply about craft. We’re looking for someone who can ramp up fast, collaborate cross-functionally, and help drive meaningful, measurable outcomes for the business.



In this role, your responsibilities will include:

 - Leading the design of product features and initiatives, from conceptualization to high-fidelity execution.

 - Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including product managers, engineers, and other designers, to define product requirements and design solutions that meet both user needs and business goals.

 - Conducting user research, usability testing, and gathering feedback to iterate and improve design solutions.

 - Designing for both web and mobile, with a focus on creating intuitive and delightful user experiences across devices.

 - Owning and evolving the visual and interaction design of the product, ensuring high-quality, pixel-perfect designs.

 - Leading design reviews and presenting design concepts to stakeholders across the company.

 - Supporting others around you, championing the company values with a positive outlook.

 - Responding effectively and respectfully to various working conditions with a growth mindset.



Required Experience:

 - 3–5 years of professional design experience.

 - Mastery in Figma and other applicable design software.

 - Relevant experience with different design methods and techniques.

 - An online or PDF portfolio (please include any passwords).



Preferred Experience:

 - Previous experience working in a Fintech startup or designing for marketplaces.

 - Previous experience facilitating research and user testing.

 - Ability to adapt well in quickly changing environments.



Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

 - An engaging social calendar, including bi-weekly catered lunches, bi-weekly “Friday bar,” team workouts, annual summer party, and holiday party, two “onsite” all-team retreats each year, semi-annual team-building events, and Hiive Women’s Network events.

 - Significant opportunities for growth into team leadership and management roles.

 - Entrepreneurial culture and a small and dynamic team.

 - Sponsorship, immigration, and relocation for exceptional candidates.



Hiive is committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where all individuals have an opportunity to succeed.

source_jobs.title: Analyst/Senior Analyst, Account Services
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a, Analyst/Senior Analyst, Account Services at Hiive, you’ll play a key role in shaping the experience of every customer who joins our platform. You’ll work alongside your team to drive iterative and scalable improvements to Hiive’s user support and onboarding experiences.



In this role, your responsibilities will include:

 - Maintaining critical operational processes to ensure efficient, compliant, and timely onboarding for Hiive users.

 - Supporting customers directly via email, providing timely product guidance and technical support.

 - Identifying and raising friction points in daily onboarding workflows and advocate for implementing process improvements that boost operational efficiency.

 - Helping refine internal processes, write documentation, and support Account Services initiatives, including building a self-serve experience for both external and internal users by writing and maintaining Hiive’s knowledge base.

 - Amplifying the voice of the customer, sharing actionable feedback with Product and Sales & Trading to advocate for enhancements.

 - Helping implement Hiive’s onboarding experience through lifecycle emails, education, and videos.



Required Skills:

 - 2-3 years of experience in a customer-facing or operational role, ideally within a SaaS or financial technology environment.

 - Solid technical aptitude and the ability to quickly learn and adapt to new tools, systems, and workflows.

 - Proactive and solution-oriented mindset, you take ownership and work with a strong sense of urgency.

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with team members.

 - Curious and detail-oriented, able to spot problems or friction in workflows and suggest potential improvements.

 - Customer-first mentality. You are focused on understanding user needs to deliver meaningful support and solutions.

 - Nice to have - experience writing and maintaining content for a customer knowledge base or lifecycle communication (e.g., email campaigns).



Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

 - An engaging social calendar, including bi-weekly catered lunches, bi-weekly “Friday bar,” team workouts, annual summer party, and holiday party, two “onsite” all-team retreats each year, semi-annual team-building events, and Hiive Women’s Network events.

 - Significant opportunities for growth into team leadership and management roles.

 - Entrepreneurial culture and a small and dynamic team.

 - Sponsorship, immigration, and relocation for exceptional candidates.

 - Identifying and raising friction points in daily onboarding workflows and advocate for implementing process improvements that boost operational efficiency.

 - Helping refine internal processes, write documentation, and support Customer Operations initiatives, including building a self-serve experience for both external and internal users by writing and maintaining Hiive’s knowledge base.

 - Amplifying the voice of the customer, sharing actionable feedback with Product and Sales & Trading to advocate for enhancements.

 - Helping implement Hiive’s onboarding experience through lifecycle emails, education, and videos.



Required Skills:

 - 2-3 years of experience in a customer-facing or operational role, ideally within a SaaS or financial technology environment.

 - Solid technical aptitude and the ability to quickly learn and adapt to new tools, systems, and workflows.

 - Proactive and solution-oriented mindset, you take ownership and work with a strong sense of urgency.

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with team members.

 - Curious and detail-oriented, able to spot problems or friction in workflows and suggest potential improvements.

 - Customer-first mentality. You are focused on understanding user needs to deliver meaningful support and solutions.

 - Nice to have - experience writing and maintaining content for a customer knowledge base or lifecycle communication (e.g., email campaigns).



Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian employees. (An employer-subsidized benefits program is available for US-based team members).

 - If you are based in Vancouver, enjoy a dedicated desk in our Vancouver, BC HQ, in the heart of downtown, with a fridge stocked with healthy snacks and drinks, an onsite gym, and a gorgeous rooftop amenity.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

 - An engaging social calendar, including bi-weekly catered lunches, bi-weekly “Friday bar,” team workouts, annual summer party, and holiday party, two “onsite” all-team retreats each year, semi-annual team-building events, and Hiive Women’s Network events.

 - Significant opportunities for growth into team leadership and management roles.

 - Entrepreneurial culture and a small and dynamic team.

 - Sponsorship, immigration, and relocation for exceptional candidates.



Hiive is committed to fostering an in

source_jobs.title: Senior Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Senior Information Security Engineer at Hiive, you'll own vulnerability management end-to-end on a three-person security team and be the security partner for every department adopting AI. Teams across the company — engineering, product, operations, finance — are rolling out AI tools and agents at an accelerating pace, and you'll make sure that happens without opening new attack surface or leaking sensitive data. You'll also keep our patching, scanning, and remediation cycles ahead of an industry curve where time-to-exploitation has collapsed from months to hours.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Triaging and coordinating remediation of vulnerabilities across SAST, SCA, DAST, CSPM, external reconnaissance, security advisories, and external bug reports on a defined SLA.

 - Tuning the existing security stack, reducing noise, and prioritizing exploitable vulnerabilities over raw severity — validating real-world exposure before remediation effort is spent.

 - Integrating LLM-based code review into the CI/CD pipeline so every change — human-written or AI-generated — gets a security review before merge.

 - Acting as the security point of contact for any department adopting AI tools, agents, MCP integrations, or custom AI/ML pipelines: reviewing proposals, flagging risks, and helping teams move forward safely.

 - Defining and maintaining practical guardrails for enterprise AI use — approved tool lists, data classification rules for AI inputs, access controls, and acceptable use policies.

 - Owning the vendor security review process end-to-end, including AI-specific assessments (data retention, model training on customer data, MCP servers, agentic tooling) and maintaining a vendor risk register.

 - Running internal penetration testing, red team exercises, and threat hunting across AWS, Kubernetes, and Docker.

 - Supporting incident response — investigation, containment, post-incident review — and deploying lightweight deception (canary tokens, honey credentials) on critical systems.

 - Maintaining asset inventory and SBOMs so we can respond quickly when new CVEs drop or coordinated disclosure waves hit.

 - Reporting vulnerability posture metrics to the CISO in business terms suitable for leadership communication.



Required Skills:

 - 3+ years of hands-on security experience spanning vulnerability management, application security, or penetration testing.

 - Operating proficiency with SAST, SCA, DAST, and external reconnaissance tooling.

 - Hands-on cloud security in AWS, with working knowledge of Kubernetes and container security.

 - Working knowledge of CI/CD pipelines and where security gates fit in the development workflow.

 - Familiarity with dependency management, SBOM generation, and software supply chain risks.

 - Willingness to use AI tools daily — coding agents, LLM-based scanners — and learn fast as the tooling evolves.

 - Clear communication: you can translate vulnerability data and AI risk into language non-technical stakeholders can act on.



Preferred Skills:

 - Experience evaluating or securing AI/ML tools in an enterprise setting, including vendor assessments, data classification for AI inputs, or writing AI acceptable use policies.

 - CISSP or OSCP certificatio

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!

As an Executive Assistant at Hiive, you will be a key partner and the ultimate force multiplier for our executive team. This isn’t a traditional gatekeeper role, you’ll be deeply embedded in our daily rhythm, handling everything from high-level project coordination, to the personal logistics that keep our founders focused on the work that moves the business forward. In a company moving as quickly as Hiive, your ability to stay ahead, work with AI-powered tools, and operate with discretion and good judgment will have a direct impact on how effectively our leadership operates.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Acting as a trusted partner to the executive team, managing their time, priorities and logistics so they can operate at their best.

 - Balancing the professional and the personal: One minute you’re organizing a high-level board deck or coordinating a business trip; the next, you’re tracking down a hard-to-get restaurant reservation, managing a home vendor, or planning a team offsite.

 - Managing correspondence, priorities, and incoming requests across multiple channels on behalf of the executive team — drafting, routing, and following up with professionalism and speed.

 - Owning complex, multi-timezone calendars across multiple leaders: anticipating conflicts, making judgement calls and coordinating directly with stakeholders to keep things moving.

 - Curating seamless travel and experiences, handling flights, accommodation, and travel coordination with creativity and an eye for a good itinerary.

 - Leading end-to-end logistics for high-stakes meetings, from venue sourcing and coordinating 20-30 meetings schedules, to building briefing materials and every detail in between.

 - Handling all travel arrangements with precision and creativity, ensuring every trip runs smoothly regardless of what comes up

 - Preparing high quality documents, presentations, and briefing materials that reflect well on the leaders you support.

 - Handling a broad range of tasks, professional and personal, with the same level of care, urgency, and discretion.

 - Keeping confidential information confidential, always.

 - Staying two steps ahead and solving problems before they become problems.

Required Skills:

 - Tech-forward: You already use AI tools (Gemini, ChatGPT, Claude) to speed up your workflows and know how to effectively prompt.

 - Exceptional organizational and time-management skills with the ability to juggle competing priorities across multiple leaders.

 - Strong written and verbal communication — polished, professional, and concise.

 - Comfortable negotiating with external parties and managing vendor relationships from sourcing through delivery.

 - Confident managing internal stakeholders at all levels, including senior leadership.

 - High proficiency in Google Workspace.

 - Demonstrated ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion.

 - Proactive, resourceful, and comfortable operating with ambiguity and minimal direction.

 - Proven experience planning and executing complex travel, domestically and internationally, with a strong eye for logistics, timing, and the unexpected.

 - Strong attention to detail and follow-through.

 - Ability to remain composed and

source_jobs.title: Senior Associate, Corporate Development
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!


As the founding Senior Associate, Corporate Development, you will be the first dedicated hire in a function that sits at the center of the company's growth strategy. Working directly with the Chief of Staff, you will evaluate M&A opportunities, manage a growing pipeline of strategic partnerships, and produce analysis and recommendations that inform how Hiive expands into new markets, products, and customer segments. The work spans due diligence, competitive research, and strategic decision support across a diverse and growing portfolio of active workstreams. This is a high-exposure role with real stakes. In any given week, you might evaluate an acquisition target, advance partnership negotiations, map a new competitive landscape, and draft a recommendation that shapes where the company invests next.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Sourcing and evaluating M&A and partnership opportunities, both by screening inbound interest and conducting proactive outbound searches, including working with sales & trading to identify strategic leads from commercial activity.

 - Managing the corporate development and partnership pipeline across active workstreams, including tracking, drafting briefs, managing follow-up cadences, and building internal alignment across stakeholders required to advance opportunities.

 - Producing strategic research that leadership can act on, including market landscape assessments, competitive analysis, and build-buy-partner recommendations for new business lines.

 - Preparing deal memos for specific M&A and partnership opportunities, including preliminary due diligence, strategic fit assessment, and key risks.

 - Supporting deal execution by coordinating with legal, finance, and product teams on diligence, terms review, and integration planning.

 - Building the operating infrastructure of the corporate development function: screening criteria, diligence templates, memo formats, and pipeline reporting.



Required Skills:

 - 3-6 years of experience in investment banking, management consulting, private equity, venture capital, startups, or corporate development.

 - Structured thinking and clear written communication. You can take a complex, ambiguous opportunity and produce a concise memo that a non-specialist can act on.

 - Ability to manage multiple concurrent workstreams with minimal oversight, balancing thoroughness with speed.

 - Fluency with AI tools for research, analysis, and document production. The Executive Office is already a strong adopter of AI across these workflows, so you will be joining a team that actively invests in these capabilities.

 - Ability to build domain knowledge quickly in a complex, regulated financial services environment. You will need to develop an understanding of pre-IPO secondary markets and institutional customer behavior, and stay current on competitive and market developments that inform Hiive's strategic decisions.

 - Comfort operating close to senior leadership in a fast-moving environment. You will regularly interact with the CEO and executive team, and be required to move confidently on emerging opportunities where the full picture is still developing.



Preferred Skills:

 - Experience at a top-tier management consulting firm

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Performance Marketing Manager at Hiive, you will own our paid acquisition engine from the ground up. You'll design, launch, and optimize campaigns across paid search and social channels, developing the infrastructure, measurement frameworks, and creative feedback loops that accelerate Hiive's paid spend from $xM to $xxM while maintaining a high ROAS. This is a hands-on role with meaningful ownership and the opportunity to shape the marketing team as Hiive grows.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Design, launch, and manage paid media campaigns across Google, Microsoft, LinkedIn, X, and other channels.

 - Own the full performance marketing stack: campaign setup, audience targeting, bid strategy, creative testing, and landing page alignment.

 - Analyze campaign performance daily, identify opportunities and inefficiencies, and continuously optimize for Revenue, ROAS, CPA, and pipeline contribution.

 - Develop and test creative briefs and messaging hypotheses in collaboration with creative to run structured A/B tests to iterate quickly.

 - Report on channel performance and paid acquisition KPIs to executives, with clear recommendations on budget allocation.

 - Stay current on platform changes, audience targeting capabilities, and emerging technologies relevant to a high-intent, financially sophisticated audience.



Required skills:

 - 5+ years of hands-on performance marketing experience, with direct ownership of paid search (Google Ads/SEM) and paid social (Meta, LinkedIn) campaigns.

 - Experience managing multi-channel paid budgets with accountability for ROAS, CPA, or pipeline contribution.

 - Comfortable pulling and interpreting data and translating it into clear budget and creative recommendations.

 - A builder mindset with a drive to constantly create new tools and processes that accelerate your work.

 - A habit of keeping up with changes in technology, Google algorithm shifts, and other developments that shape the way you work.



Preferred skills:

 - Familiarity with B2B or ABM-style paid campaigns targeting financial advisors, family offices, or institutional audiences.

 - Working knowledge of data analysis tools like Omni, Looker, PostHog, Tableau, etc.

 - Experience in fintech, financial services, or a regulated industry, or marketing to an affluent/professional audience.



Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Enjoy a $20-per-day commuter benefit for every day you work in our Vancouver HQ.

source_jobs.title: QA Manager
source_jobs.description:
Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.



Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies and backed by leading U.S. investors, Hiive is profitable, well-capitalized, and building a high-performance team to meet growing demand and pursue new market opportunities.



Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a QA Manager at Hiive, you will lead a team of 3 QA engineers and own quality across our platform. You will define the QA strategy, own the test infrastructure and processes that allow Hiive to ship with confidence, and evolve how the team operates as AI-assisted engineering reshapes the way our product is built. This is a leadership role where you'll shape both the people and the practice of quality at Hiive.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Lead, mentor, and develop a team of 3 QA engineers, setting clear ownership areas, performance expectations, and career growth paths.

 - Define and own the QA strategy end-to-end — from test planning and case design through to reporting and continuous improvement — and adapt it continuously to the changing needs of AI-enabled product engineering.

 - Design and maintain an automated test framework covering Hiive's core transaction, onboarding, and platform workflows.

 - Integrate QA into CI/CD pipelines so that quality gates are automated and regression coverage runs on every build.

 - Partner closely with product and engineering to review requirements, identify testability issues early, and advocate for quality throughout the development lifecycle.

 - Track and report on defect trends, test coverage metrics, team performance, and release readiness to engineering leadership.

 - Triage and investigate production issues, coordinating root cause analysis and ensuring fixes are covered by regression tests.

 - Evaluate and introduce new tooling, AI-assisted testing approaches, or processes as Hiive's platform and team scale.



Required skills:

 - 5+ years of software QA experience, with at least 2 years managing QA engineers.

 - Demonstrated experience defining and driving QA strategy.

 - Experience evolving QA practices for AI-assisted engineering workflows.

 - Strong hands-on experience with test automation frameworks — Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, or equivalent.

 - Experience integrating automated tests into CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, or similar).

 - Solid understanding of testing methodologies: functional, regression, integration, and end-to-end.

 - Ability to write clear test plans, test cases, and quality reports for both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment where you define the process, not just follow it.



Preferred skills:

 - Experience evolving QA practices in environments where engineers use AI coding assistants (Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, or similar) in their daily work.

 - Experience in fintech, marketplace platforms, or other regulated software environments.

 - Familiarity with performance testing or security testing practices.

 - Experience with API testing tools such as Postman or REST-Assured.

 - Background working on small engineering teams where QA and engineering collaborate closely.
   
   

Compensation, Benefits & Perks:

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.

 - Opportunity to participate in ownership of a rapidly growing company through our employee stock option plan.

 - Comprehensive 100% employer-paid health & dental premiums and a Health/Personal Spending Account for Canadian emplo

Hiive is redefining how private companies and their shareholders access liquidity. Through its institutional-grade platform, Hiive brings together buyers, sellers, and issuers to facilitate secondary transactions in venture-backed, pre-IPO companies, introducing efficiency, transparency, and standardization to an otherwise opaque asset class.

Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As Hiive’s Director, Communications, you will define and lead the company’s communications strategy across external media, executive visibility, social channels, and internal communications. You’ll own how Hiive is understood by the market — shaping our narrative across investors, founders, employees, shareholders, and the broader startup ecosystem.



This is a highly strategic and hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling Hiive’s communications function. You’ll oversee media relations, executive communications, thought leadership, brand storytelling, corporate messaging, and internal communications while partnering closely with leadership, product marketing, growth, legal, and design teams.



You’ll play a critical role in elevating Hiive’s brand, strengthening market positioning, and building trust and credibility as we scale.



In this role, your responsibilities would include:

 - Own and lead Hiive’s end-to-end communications strategy across PR, corporate communications, executive communications, social media, and internal communications.

 - Define and evolve the company narrative, messaging architecture, and brand voice across all external and internal touchpoints.

 - Serve as a strategic advisor to company leadership on communications strategy, reputation management, media positioning, and executive visibility.

 - Build and manage relationships with media, journalists, influencers, and external partners to drive high-impact earned media coverage.

 - Lead executive communications and thought leadership initiatives, including press, speaking opportunities, social presence, and industry engagement.

 - Own Hiive’s social media strategy and presence across LinkedIn, X/Twitter, and emerging channels to amplify brand awareness and engagement.

 - Develop integrated communications campaigns that support product launches, company milestones, partnerships, funding announcements, and strategic initiatives.

 - Lead internal communications efforts to ensure alignment, clarity, and consistency across the organization as the company scales.

 - Identify and execute sponsorships, partnerships, and thought leadership opportunities that strengthen Hiive’s market presence and credibility.

 - Support industry events through media outreach, executive speaking engagements, and coordinated communications strategies.

 - Partner cross-functionally with product marketing, growth, design, legal, and leadership teams to ensure consistency and effectiveness across all messaging.

 - Build scalable communications processes, frameworks, and measurement systems to evaluate brand and communications impact.



Required Skills:

 - 10+ years of experience in communications, PR, corporate communications, brand storytelling, or content strategy, ideally within fintech, marketplaces, SaaS, venture-backed startups, or high-growth technology companies.

 - Proven experience leading and scaling communications functions in fast-paced, high-growth environments.

 - Exceptional storytelling, writing, and editorial instincts with the ability to simplify complex financial and marketplace concepts into compelling narratives.

 - Strong track record managing media relations, executive communications, thought leadership, crisis

source_jobs.description:

Interested in learning more about life at Hiive? Check out our careers page https://www.hiive.com/careers to see how you can grow with us!



As a Senior Product Designer at Hiive, you’ll join a fast-growing, high-impact product team. You will combine design craft, analytical thinking and user behaviour knowledge with an AI technology stack to define and deliver the user experience end to end. We’re looking for someone who can ramp up fast, collaborate cross-functionally, and help drive meaningful, measurable outcomes for the business.



In this role, your responsibilities will include:

 - Leading the design and implementation of key product features and initiatives, from conceptualization through iteration and delivery in code using AI agents (primarily Claude) to prototype and build interfaces.

 - Collaborating with engineers in delivering front-end code for new features and optimizing the user experience based on user feedback.

 - Collaborating with product managers, business stakeholders, and other designers, to refine product requirements and design solutions that meet both user needs and business goals.

 - Conducting user research, usability testing, and gathering feedback to iterate and improve UX solutions.

 - Designing and delivering web and mobile interfaces, with a focus on creating intuitive and delightful user experiences across devices.

 - Owning and evolving the user experience output of a product team in combination with engineering.

 - Presenting design concepts, prototypes and production demos to stakeholders across the company. Fielding questions and engaging in detailed conversations about the user experience, development, and business ramifications of a design.

 - Supporting others around you, championing the company values with a positive outlook.

 - Responding effectively and respectfully to various working conditions with a growth mindset.
   
   

Required Experience:

 - 5+ years of professional design experience.

 - Experience working with Claude Code, Claude Design or equivalent AI tooling for producing user interfaces.

 - Experience facilitating research and user testing.

 - Experience working with and developing design systems.

 - Experience collaborating with development to push production code.

 - An online or PDF portfolio (please include any passwords).



Preferred Experience:

 - Previous experience working in a Fintech startup or designing for marketplaces.

 - Front-end engineering experience in React.

 - Highly competitive salary commensurate with experience and contribution.
